_notes: Hand-curated by walking the US Core Location differential (10 elements) against the R4 Location snapshot (39 elements). Verified with PowerShell. Covers: 1 cardinality increase (name 0->1), new binding on address.state, reference profile narrowing (managingOrganization), and MustSupport additions. Note: R4 Location.status has a required binding but no version pin in R4 snapshot (unlike Patient.gender which had |4.0.1); US Core differential does not re-state the binding, so no binding version change.

drifts.description: MustSupport added in target (min=0, optional but must be supported when present).
Cardinality changed: [0..1] -> [1..1]. Location name becomes mandatory in US Core; R4 allows a location without a name.
MustSupport added in target (min becomes 1 -> conformance-critical).
MustSupport added in target (min=0, optional but must be supported when present).
MustSupport added in target (min=0, optional but must be supported when present).
MustSupport added in target (min=0, optional but must be supported when present). R4 Location snapshot does not constrain address sub-elements.
MustSupport added in target (min=0, optional but must be supported when present). R4 Location snapshot does not constrain address sub-elements.
MustSupport added in target (min=0, optional but must be supported when present). R4 Location snapshot does not constrain address sub-elements.
New binding added in target: extensible binding to http://hl7.org/fhir/us/core/ValueSet/us-core-usps-state (US USPS state codes). R4 Location base does not constrain address.state with any binding.
MustSupport added in target (min=0, optional but must be supported when present). R4 Location snapshot does not constrain address sub-elements.
MustSupport added in target (min=0, optional but must be supported when present).
Reference target profile constrained: R4 allows Reference(Organization). US Core restricts to Reference(us-core-organization).
